title: QA Failure Density Enumeration PASS — sampling-comparison variance-decay demonstration on satellite class across 3 seeds × 3 N levels per (Kochenderfer, 2026) eq. 7.3
_fixture_purpose: Secondary PASS fixture: focuses the FDE_SAMPLING + FDE_STDERR gates on the mid-rare 'satellite' class (p_true = 8/81 ≈ 0.0988) across three independent seeds (42, 1337, 2024) and three sample sizes (100, 1000, 10000). Per (Kochenderfer, 2026) eq. 7.3 the empirical estimator standard error decays as sqrt(p(1-p)/N) ≈ 0.0298 / 0.00943 / 0.00298 across the three N. All nine seeded cases land inside the 4-sigma envelope, while the QA enumeration error remains identically 0. This decoupled fixture lets reviewers see the variance-decay pattern without the multi-class noise of the primary fixture.
source_attribution: (Kochenderfer, 2026) Algorithms for Validation §7.1 direct-estimation MLE p̂_fail = n/m and eq. 7.3 sigma_hat = sqrt(p (1 - p) / N); anchored at docs/theory/kochenderfer_validation_excerpts.md#val-7-1-direct-estimation-pfail. Cert [194] qa_cognition_space_morphospace_cert_v1 supplies the mod-9 orbit-class definitions (Sole, 2026; arxiv:2601.12837 via Dale, 2026). Bridge spec: docs/specs/QA_KOCHENDERFER_BRIDGE.md §3. Authored Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-27.

exact_enumeration.method: Same enumeration as primary fixture; declared here for FDE_RATIO completeness; the load-bearing claim of this fixture is the FDE_SAMPLING / FDE_STDERR gate, not FDE_RATIO.

variance_decay_demonstration.decay_ratio_check: sigma scales as 1/sqrt(N): sigma_100 / sigma_10000 = sqrt(100) = 10

claim: Across 3 independent seeds {42, 1337, 2024} and 3 sample sizes {100, 1000, 10000}, the seeded direct-sampling estimator p̂ for the satellite orbit class on S_9 falls within the |error| <= 4 * sigma envelope predicted by Kochenderfer eq. 7.3, while QA enumeration produces 8/81 exactly with variance 0. This is the load-bearing falsifiable empirical claim distinguishing this cert from a definition cert.
claim_scope: This cert does not prove that QA enumeration is novel as probability theory; it proves that an existing QA reachability cert ([194]) can be re-expressed in Kochenderfer's validation vocabulary and gains an exact finite-state estimator with zero sampling variance, while the corresponding direct estimator exhibits the expected Bernoulli sampling error.

witnesses.kochenderfer_role: minority anchor — included for FDE_WIT completeness
load-bearing class — the variance-decay sweep targets this class
majority anchor — included for FDE_WIT completeness
